mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher near-term corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases during early-cycle phases, or predictive alpha decays to zero after controlling for momentum.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and restrictive monetary policy creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The dual-signal regime yields positive 60-day returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases the denominator in valuation multiples and expands corporate profit pools, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which triggers immediate positive equity repricing and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 150,000 are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes after adjusting for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and retail sales expand concurrently, it confirms a healthy balance between supply capacity and consumer demand, reducing inventory risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The concurrent expansion signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons, with cumulative alpha below 0.5% per year in walk-forward testing.
